summary:
In CLL/SLL, tumor cells originate primarily from mature B-cells based on their characteristic surface markers like CD5+CD19+, observed clonal expansions involving activated but fully differentiated B-cell populations, alongside specific mutational profiles affecting post-maturation stages only.
The summary focuses on highlighting how tumor cell origins are linked directly back to mature B-lymphocytes through multiple supportive evidences including morphologic characteristics, patterns of proliferation, and molecular genetics findings pertinent specifically within context of Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia / Small Lymphocyte Lymphomas conditions. Agent2 concluded its analysis emphasizing the derivation of cancerous growths strictly originating amongst already developed immune system components without any mention outside those parameters being considered plausible sources according to current medical literature reviews conducted during examination process. 

Key Points Summarized:

Tumor cells in CLL/SLL arise mainly from mature B-cells due to distinctive marker expressions indicating full development stage; they also show signs via genomic alterations targeting late-stage differentiation processes exclusively associated solely between said hematopoietic lineage transformations culminating into neoplasia seen clinically manifesting under given disease states definitions outlined herein.
